label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is asking if following a set of instructions will bring you back to the starting point. The instructions are: Always face forward. Take 2 steps right. Take 4 steps right. Take 6 steps left. The options are yes or no. First, I need to visualize this. The key here is to track the net movement in the right-left direction. 1. Start by facing forward. The direction you're facing (forward) isn't specified in terms of cardinal directions (north, east, etc.), but since the movements are described as right and left, I assume "right" and "left" are relative to the direction you're facing. So if you're facing forward, "right" would be to your right and "left" would be to your left. 2. Take 2 steps right. This moves you 2 units to the right from the starting point. 3. Take 4 steps right. This moves you another 4 units to the right. So total movement to the right is 2 + 4 = 6 units to the right. 4. Take 6 steps left. This moves you 6 units to the left. So the net movement is: 6 units right minus 6 units left = 0 units. So you end up at the starting point. But wait, I need to make sure I didn't misinterpret "right" and "left". Let me double check. If "right" means moving in a specific direction relative to your facing direction, and you never changed direction (you always faced forward), then "right" and "left" are consistent directions.
